summary.intent: Address an open Codex code review on PR #705 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically a correctness issue in the Marquee component's SyncProps logic around interaction pause handling.
summary.outcome: Fixed SyncProps to compute remaining interaction pause causes before deciding whether to resume animation, added two regression tests, updated the spec to match, and committed the fix with all gates (tests, fmt, clippy, spec validation) passing.

repo: Marquee pause state is multi-causal: hover and focus can each independently hold a pause, so SyncProps must count remaining causes after clearing a prop rather than resuming unconditionally.
The spec at spec/components/data-display/marquee.md must stay in sync with implementation logic; the remaining-pause-cause rule needed a prose update alongside the code fix.
CI gates run in sequence: cargo test → cargo fmt --check → cargo xtask spec compile-snippets → cargo clippy → cargo xtask spec validate.

code.finding: SyncProps logic; original bug was deciding resume state before evaluating which pause causes remain after clearing interaction flags.

Regression test location for marquee prop-sync behavior, including the two new tests: resumes when clearing only interaction pause policy, and stays paused when clearing one of multiple interaction causes.
Spec section describing SyncProps / remaining-pause-cause rule; updated to match the fixed implementation.

workflow: Use `gh api graphql` with a targeted jq filter to fetch only unresolved Codex review threads, avoiding noise from resolved or human threads.
Write a failing regression test first to confirm the review finding before patching — the test failure itself validates the bug path.
Stage only the two review-fix files explicitly (not `git add .`) to keep the commit scope tight.

friction: The initial SyncProps implementation had a subtle ordering bug (state decision before cause evaluation) that only manifested when multiple pause causes were active — not caught by prior tests.
open_items: Reply to the Codex review thread and request a re-review — mentioned as the intended next step but not shown as completed in the transcript.
